description: a locust (as pointed, or as lighting on the top of vegetation)
origin: apparently from the same as g0206
notes: numberless swarms of them almost every spring are carried by the wind from arabia into palestine, and having devastated that country, migrate to regions farther north, until they perish by falling into the sea. middle easterners were accustomed to feed upon locusts, either raw or roasted and seasoned with salt (or prepared in other ways), and the israelites also were permitted to eat them.
